title: Architecture Evaluation, Quality Attributes, and Decision Governance · Product Forensics
summary: A preserved decision record for architecture evaluation, quality attributes, and decision governance contains one plausible evidence, scope, design, implementation, evaluation, or ownership defect. Find the first unsupported claim before changing the product. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in evaluated architecture and decision governance packet and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case…
objectives: Describe context, containers, components, data, deployment, runtime, trust, operations, and ownership views at useful levels.
Analyze concrete quality-attribute scenarios and trade accessibility, security, privacy, reliability, performance, modifiability, cost, and simplicity explicitly.
Define failure domains, resource bounds, dependency timeouts, degraded modes, safe stop, recovery, and blast-radius evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Architecture Viewpoints
steps.instruction:
debug case — A box diagram omits quality tradeoffs, trust and failure boundaries, cost, evolution, dissent, review triggers, and rejected alternatives. The debug variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Describe context, containers, components, data, deployment, runtime, trust, operations, and ownership views at useful levels.

Inspect the Architecture Viewpoints trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Architecture Viewpoints.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for describe context, containers, components, data, deployment, runtime, trust, operations, and ownership views at useful levels. For Inspect the failure record · Debug Architecture Viewpoints, keep Architecture Viewpoints connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: For Architecture Viewpoints, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Inspect the failure record · Debug Architecture Viewpoints, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Architecture Viewpoints precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Inspect the failure record · Debug Architecture Viewpoints, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A box diagram omits quality tradeoffs, trust and failure boundaries, cost, evolution, dissent, review triggers, and rejected alternatives. The debug variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Describe context, containers, components, data, deployment, runtime, trust, operations, and ownership views at useful levels.

For Inspect the failure record · Debug Architecture Viewpoints, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: One box-and-arrow diagram explains an architecture.

stimulus.title: Architecture Viewpoints case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A box diagram omits quality tradeoffs, trust and failure boundaries, cost, evolution, dissent, review triggers, and rejected alternatives. The debug variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Describe context, containers, components, data, deployment, runtime, trust, operations, and ownership views at useful levels.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: One box-and-arrow diagram explains an architecture.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Describe context, containers, components, data, deployment, runtime, trust, operations, and ownership views at useful levels. Artifact under review: evaluated architecture and decision governance packet.

options.text: The Architecture Viewpoints record says evaluated architecture and decision governance packet looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect the failure record evidence boundary for Architecture Viewpoints.

For inspect the failure record, apply Architecture Viewpoints without its stated preconditions, omit the evaluated architecture and decision governance packet trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Architecture Viewpoints for evaluated architecture and decision governance packet.
The record names the assumption, applies Architecture Viewpoints, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Architecture Review
steps.instruction:
Append a capstone-architecture-review-rwwckk: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Architecture Review.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Architecture Review into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Architecture Review, keep Architecture Review connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.code:
capstone-architecture-review-rwwckk:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Architecture Review structural check is only one gate. evaluated architecture and decision governance packet must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

hints: For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Architecture Review, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Architecture Review precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Architecture Review, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A box diagram omits quality tradeoffs, trust and failure boundaries, cost, evolution, dissent, review triggers, and rejected alternatives. The debug variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Run a structured review with scenarios, alternatives, evidence gaps, dissent, decisions, owners, and timed re-evaluation.

For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Architecture Review, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Architecture review is a presentation seeking approval.

steps.title: Publish the incident record · Debug Architecture Review
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Architecture Review: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A box diagram omits quality tradeoffs, trust and failure boundaries, cost, evolution, dissent, review triggers, and rejected alternatives. The debug variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Run a structured review with scenarios, alternatives, evidence gaps, dissent, decisions, owners, and timed re-evaluation.

Evidence target: Publish the incident record · Debug Architecture Review.

For Publish the incident record · Debug Architecture Review, keep Architecture Review connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Run a structured review with scenarios, alternatives, evidence gaps, dissent, decisions, owners, and timed re-evaluation.
